title: Overcoming self sabotage habits. #podcast #interview #quotes #advice #speech #motivation #talk
description:
Your excuses end today.

Matthew McConaughey delivers a lesson many people avoid: self-forgiveness means nothing if you keep repeating the same mistakes. 

He explains how he used to forgive himself every Sunday, only to go back to the same habits Monday through Friday. 

That cycle became a trap, a comfortable excuse to stay the same.

At some point, he realized the truth: change only happens when you stop giving yourself a parachute. 

No more "I'll do better next week." 

No more hiding behind grace, luck, or second chances. He looked in the mirror and took ownership.

The message is simple. your life doesn't shift until you do.

McConaughey's mindset is a call to stop drifting and start driving. 

Put your hands on the wheel. Hold yourself to the standard you know you're capable of. 

Responsibility is not punishment, it's freedom.

If this hit you, share it with someone who needs this wake-up call today.

Speaker: @officiallymcconaughey
Credit: @thediaryofaceopodcast

title: Markus Flasch, CEO BMW Motorrad. #bmwmotorrad #motorrad  #podcast #interview #tomorrowthinking
description:
Was bedeutet Freiheit, wenn jedes Lebensbereich zunehmend reglementiert, vermessen und digitalisiert wird? In dieser Folge von Tomorrow Thinking spricht Künstler und Kurator Johann Alexis von Haehling mit Markus Flasch – CEO BMW Motorrad, zuvor CEO der BMW M GmbH und einer der profiliertesten Manager der internationalen Automobil- und Motorradindustrie.

Markus Flasch gehört zu den seltenen Stimmen in der Industrie, die ihre Produktstrategie nicht aus Trendreports ableiten, sondern aus einer tiefen kulturellen Lesart ihrer Kundschaft. Wir sprechen über die Frage, warum BMW Motorrad das Elektrothema bewusst nicht erzwingt, über das Motorrad als Lifestyleprodukt jenseits reiner Mobilität, über Risiko und Eigenverantwortung als gesellschaftliche Werte – und über die Frage, warum gerade Motorradfahren ein Resonanzraum für Realität, Natur und körperliche Präsenz ist, in einer Welt, die immer weiter ins Digitale abrutscht.

Es geht um Markenführung, Designphilosophie, die Kultur der individuellen Mobilität und um die schwierige Realität einer Gesellschaft, die zwischen Sicherheit und Freiheit, Regulierung und Selbstbestimmung neu austarieren muss.

Wie immer sprechen wir auch über Kunst. Im Atelier von Johann Alexis diskutieren die beiden die fast bildhauerische Begegnung zwischen menschlichem Körper und Maschine – die Fusion aus amorphem, organischem Körper und technischer Erweiterung – und über Wüstenlandschaften, Geräusche und sensorische Realität als wiederkehrende Themen in Johann Alexis’ Malerei.

Podcast Newsletter & Website: www.tomorrow-thinking.com

Johann Alexis Kunst: www.johannalexis.com

Producer: Malcolm Gompf

title: "We don't know what we don't know" #onlinesafety #podcast #interview
description:
A clip from "This Isn't a Ban. It's a RE-BEL-UTION".

What if the answer to the UK's social media ban debate isn't a ban at all?
Meet Munur Shah, author of The Screen Addict and founder of Rebel Telecom, launching June 2025, and he's about to spark a RE-BEL-UTION.
While politicians debate whether to ban under-16s from social media, Munur is doing something far more radical. He's building a different kind of phone. A different kind of solution. One rooted not in restriction, but in education, awareness, and breaking the cycle of addiction at its source.
And he's not doing it alone.
Joining us in this conversation is Laura Bloomberg, an independent consultant with over three decades of teaching experience, specialising in dyslexia and dyscalculia. Laura works alongside local authorities, schools, and families to dismantle the barriers that prevent children with specific learning difficulties from thriving, and her life's work is built on a simple but powerful belief: that every child deserves to be supported, understood, and celebrated. She has been working closely with Munur on the development of Rebel Telecom, helping to build bridges with educational establishments and raise awareness of what a healthier, safer relationship with technology could look like for our young people. She's here because she believes in this rebellion, and her expertise brings a vital dimension to the conversation.
In this episode of Voices on the Edge of Change, we unpack the full story.
As a coach and NLP specialist, Munur has spent years helping people understand and overcome screen addiction, and the work is deeply personal, deeply practical, and urgently needed. We explore what addiction really looks like in the digital age, why willpower alone will never be enough, and why the conversation we're having as a society is missing something critical.
Then there's Rebel Telecom. Designed with the very audiences who would be affected by a blanket ban in mind, the Rebel Phone offers a genuine alternative, one that asks us to think differently about how we hand technology to our young people, and what we're really trying to protect them from.
This is not a conversation about taking phones away. It's a conversation about waking up.
Whether you're a parent, an educator, a policy maker, or simply someone who's noticed how much time you spend staring at a screen, this episode will make you think. And it might just change what you do next.
🔔 Subscribe so you never miss an episode
💬 What do you think, education or legislation? Drop it in the comments below
Voices on the Edge of Change is hosted by Lou Robey, LinkedIn Top Voice on Change, business transformation expert, and founder of Voice4Impact.

Most decorated female rescue swimmer!
She didn't demand respect. She earned it.
The first female ROMEO Chief shares how she navigated disrespect from male sailors early in her career: by outworking them, outsmarting them, and proving she belonged.
No speeches. No complaints. No waiting for the culture to catch up. She just got to work and let her performance silence the doubt.

And how she turned all of that into fuel.
She talks about the mindset that carried her through, the leadership lessons she learned the hard way, and what she'd tell the next generation of female sailors stepping into spaces where they're still the first.
A reminder that competence is the loudest answer to doubt.
